title: Explanation of Control Structure for Practical Fuel Economy Improvement - Effective Control

blocks.text: Explanation of Control Structure for Practical Fuel Economy Improvement - Effective Control
In addition to the normal driving mode (ECON OFF mode), this vehicle has an ECON ON mode that provides specialized control to improve practical fuel economy.
In ECON ON mode, the PGM-FI system, the CVT control system, and the air conditioning system are "effectively controlled" to improve fuel economy.

When the driver operates the ECON switch in the ECON OFF mode, the signal is input to the combination meter, which sends a signal to the PGM-FI ECU and the A/C control unit via the F-CAN and B-CAN to switch to the ECON ON mode. This signal is sent from the combination meter to the PGM-FI ECU and A/C control unit via F-CAN and B-CAN. In accordance with this signal, each unit switches to effective control. At this time, the PGM-FI ECU sends a signal through F-CAN to the combination meter to turn on the ECON indicator light. When the driver operates the ECON switch while in ECON ON mode (during effective control), the ECON ON mode is changed to ECON OFF mode. The signal flow at this time is the same as when shifting from the ECON OFF mode to the ECON ON mode, and each system that receives the signal aborts effectivity control. The ECON ON/OFF mode status is stored in the combination meter and is retained even if the ignition is turned to the OFF (LOCK) mode.

In ECON OFF mode, the throttle valve is gently controlled to reduce fuel consumption when the accelerator pedal is depressed roughly. In addition, the throttle opening is gentler than in the ECON OFF mode. However, if the accelerator pedal is depressed beyond a certain level, the throttle is automatically controlled in the same manner as in the ECON OFF mode to ensure acceleration performance.
